title: rubus × implacitus
intro: Rubus × implacitus, also known as a hybrid black raspberry or black raspberry, is a plant species that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae) and is a cross between Rubus laciniatus and Rubus occidentalis. The medicinal properties of Rubus × implacitus include its high content of anthocyanins, which are powerful antioxidants that have anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of consuming Rubus × implacitus include reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, improving cardiovascular health, and potentially aiding in the prevention of certain cancers and neurodegenerative diseases. Rubus × implacitus can be prepared as a juice, tea, or supplement, and is also used in jams, jellies, and other food products, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects on human health.
